mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound compresses the equity discount rate on a persistent basis, mechanically inflating the present value of future cash flows and producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns as long as FEDFUNDS remains below 1%.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window while FEDFUNDS remains below 1%, or positive-return hit rate during near-zero-rate periods is not statistically distinguishable from the unconditional 65.5% base rate (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above a 9% threshold while nonfarm payrolls continue contracting, equity markets have already priced maximum labor-market pessimism, so subsequent 60-day forward returns are positive as markets front-run eventual stabilization ('bad news is priced, recovery anticipated').
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=9.5% and PAYEMS declining as of 2009-06-01 vintage
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns following UNRATE>9% crossings with contracting payrolls are negative or indistinguishable from zero in more than 40% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A joint turn where industrial production stabilizes (month-over-month decline decelerates) while retail sales simultaneously stop contracting signals a synchronized demand-supply inflection that jointly confirms trough formation; this combined signal predicts positive 20-day forward equity returns more reliably than either series alone because it rules out inventory-driven false signals.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month decline decelerates for 2 consecutive months AND RSAFS month-over-month change turns non-negative
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO and RSAFS both showing decelerating declines mid-2009
regime_hint: recession trough transitioning to early recovery
failure_condition: Joint INDPRO+RSAFS stabilization signal produces forward 20d positive-return hit rate not exceeding the single-series INDPRO-only signal by a statistically significant margin, or interaction term coefficient is not significant (p > 0.10) in a logistic regression against direction_20d.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts bottoming after a multi-year collapse acts as a leading indicator of broader demand recovery specific to post-financial-crisis regimes, because residential investment is typically the first component of GDP to turn during a credit-driven recession; a rise in HOUST from cycle lows predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter only in regimes following a housing-led recession.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 532-540k units in mid-2009, near historic lows
failure_condition: HOUST upturns from cycle lows fail to precede positive 60-day forward returns in at least 55% of instances during housing-crisis-driven recessions, or the effect is not present when isolating post-2008 data specifically.

mechanism_narrative: Short-horizon momentum in the rate of change of nonfarm payroll losses (i.e., smaller month-over-month declines) is continuously incorporated into near-term equity sentiment, producing a persistent positive drift in 5-day forward S&P 500 returns whenever the payroll decline is decelerating, independent of any threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS declining but at a decelerating pace into mid-2009
failure_condition: 5-day forward return correlation with PAYEMS month-over-month deceleration is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over rolling 1-year windows, or sign flips persist for more than 2 consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI year-over-year inflation falls below 0% (deflation threshold crossed), equity markets react negatively over the subsequent 20 trading days because deflation signals demand collapse and raises real debt burdens, offsetting any benefit from low nominal rates.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage value 214.459 in June 2009 amid disinflationary pressure
failure_condition: During CPI YoY negative-print periods, forward 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the negative-return effect disappears when controlling for concurrent FEDFUNDS easing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising retail sales alongside a still-elevated but plateauing unemployment rate indicates that consumer demand is recovering ahead of labor market lag, a combination that historically precedes sustained quarterly equity rallies more strongly than either signal in isolation because it decouples consumption recovery from employment-driven fear.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS and UNRATE both showing signs of stabilization by mid-2009
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS-up/UNRATE-plateau signal fails to outperform the univariate RSAFS-only signal in forward 60-day hit rate by a statistically significant margin (bootstrap p > 0.10), falsifying the interaction claim.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where industrial production has fallen more than 10% from its pre-recession peak and shows month-over-month stabilization, short-horizon equity returns are positively biased because markets treat trough formation in the supply side of the economy as a leading recovery signal specific to deep-recession regimes, unlike in mild slowdowns where INDPRO stabilization carries no such signal.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO down sharply from peak by mid-2009 (95.3964 vintage, later revised to 84.7459)
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO stabilization in deep-recession regimes (>10% peak-to-trough decline) do not exceed the unconditional 65.5% positive base rate by a statistically significant margin, or the effect is absent when tested on the 2001 and 1990 recession analogues.
